raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing this complaint regarding Citis handling of a billing dispute that was denied multiple times despite clear merchant nonperformance. 

I purchased two nightstands from XXXX using my Citi credit card. After delivery and assembly, both items were visibly damaged and scratched. I immediately contacted XXXX customer service and spoke with a manager, who acknowledged the damage. The manager offered a partial discount if I kept the items, which I declined because I purchased new furniture and did not want damaged merchandise. 

XXXX then arranged for a freight pickup to retrieve the damaged items due to their size and weight. A pickup was scheduled, but no carrier arrived. I contacted XXXX again and was told another pickup would be arranged ; again, no one came. Despite repeated attempts on my part, XXXX failed to retrieve the defective merchandise. 

Importantly, I remained within XXXX return window throughout this process. The return deadline had not expired, and I can provide a screenshot showing that the order was still eligible for return. XXXX  failure to retrieve the items prevented me from completing the return. 

Citi denied my dispute, classifying the issue as a manufacturers warranty matter. This is incorrect. I am not seeking repair or replacement under a warranty. This dispute involves defective merchandise and the merchants failure to perform an agreed return pickup. 

I acted in good faith and made reasonable efforts to resolve the issue directly with the merchant. Citis denial reflects a misapplication of chargeback protections related to merchant nonperformance. I am requesting executive-level review and appropriate correction of this billing decision.
